Title: Redirecting tropane alkaloid metabolism reveals pyrrolidine alkaloid diversity in Atropa belladonna

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: TRV2 VIGS silencing construct; inducible/virus-based silencing system
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 4-(1-methyl-2-pyrrolidinyl)-3-oxobutanoic acid precursor leading to tropinone; supports tropane alkaloid formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of the polyketide intermediate used to generate 4-(1-methyl-2-pyrrolidinyl)-3-oxobutanoic acid, channeling N-methyl-Δ1-pyrrolinium into tropane alkaloid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Diverts flux toward target product and away from alternative pyrrolidine alkaloid formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Tropane alkaloids include pharmacologically important compounds such as hyoscyamine and scopolamine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing reduces tropane alkaloid abundance and causes accumulation of N-methyl-Δ1-pyrrolinium cation and many novel pyrrolidine alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key enzyme in tropane alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Virus-induced gene silencing; root tissue analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS silencing in A. belladonna roots; LC-MS discovery metabolomics; targeted metabolite profiling; tandem MS
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced flux to tropane alkaloids with strong metabolic redirection toward off-target pyrrolidine alkaloid diversity.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics (LC-MS, LC-MS/MS); data available on request
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Silencing caused accumulation of nearly 40 pyrrolidine alkaloids, revealing an alternative metabolic network driven partly by nonenzymatic chemistry.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AbPyKS is a central tropane-pathway enzyme in A. belladonna roots. 2. Silencing AbPyKS decreases tropane alkaloids including scopolamine-, hyoscyamine/littorine-, tropinone-, and tropanol-related metabolites. 3. Loss of AbPyKS activity increases N-methyl-Δ1-pyrrolinium and uncovers a pyrrolidine alkaloid subnetwork. 4. Metabolic redirection enabled discovery of P1/P2- and P4/P5-type pyrrolidines formed from 2-O-malonylphenyllactate and the pyrrolinium cation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful lever for redirecting tropane flux and discovering new alkaloid scaffolds, but suppression reduces desired medicinal tropane alkaloids and creates many off-target products.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: TRV2 VIGS silencing construct in A. belladonna; pEAQ-HT transient expression vector in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenyllactate supply branch feeding aromatic tropane ester and pyrrolidine alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces phenylpyruvate to phenyllactic acid, supplying precursor for 2-O-malonylphenyllactate and downstream pyrrolidine/tropane-related metabolites.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Pyrrolidine alkaloids derived from P1/P2 scaffold; aromatic tropane alkaloid branch
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes precursor PLA used in medicinal tropane ester biosynthesis and novel pyrrolidine alkaloid formation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots in A. belladonna; leaves in transient N. benthamiana assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing decreases PLA, 2-O-malonylphenyllactate, and pyrrolidines P1, P2, and P6; coexpression enables production of P1/P2 in N. benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key precursor-supply step for PLA-derived alkaloid formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Analyzed in roots; transiently expressed in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Virus-induced gene silencing in A. belladonna; transient expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS silencing with qRT-PCR validation; targeted LC-MS metabolite analysis; transient expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing AbPPAR lowers precursor pools for both desired aromatic tropane alkaloids and newly uncovered pyrrolidines.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics (LC-MS, LC-MS/MS); qRT-PCR; data available on request
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AbPPAR is ~10-fold less catalytically efficient with 4-hydroxyphenylpyruvate than with phenylpyruvate, suggesting some substrate promiscuity relevant to hydroxylated pyrrolidines.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AbPPAR catalyzes phenylpyruvate to PLA conversion. 2. VIGS of AbPPAR reduced transcript abundance by 79% and lowered PLA and 2-O-malonylphenyllactate. 3. P1, P2, and P6 decreased in AbPPAR-silenced roots, supporting PLA derivation. 4. Coexpression with AbArAT4, AbPMT2, and AbMPO2 enabled P1/P2 production in N. benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A practical engineering target for controlling PLA-derived alkaloid flux; should be coordinated with upstream aminotransferase and pyrrolinium-forming genes for scaffold production.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: PLA precursor-supply branch linked to tropane and pyrrolidine alkaloid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers the amino group from phenylalanine to 4-hydroxyphenylpyruvate/related keto acid partner to generate phenylpyruvate, feeding PLA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supplies aromatic precursor for formation of PLA-derived pyrrolidine scaffolds.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves in transient N. benthamiana assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Together with AbPPAR, AbPMT2, and AbMPO2 enables production of P1 and P2 in N. benthamiana by supplying PLA precursor.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression in N. benthamiana followed by LC-MS and MS/MS co-elution analysis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promiscuity may also enable formation of 4-hydroxyphenylpyruvate-derived products, potentially broadening byproducts.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics (LC-MS, LC-MS/MS); data available on request
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported to transfer amino group from tyrosine to phenylpyruvate to yield phenylalanine and 4-hydroxyphenylpyruvate, but is ~250-fold less efficient than with phenylalanine as amino donor.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AbArAT4 contributes upstream precursor formation for PLA biosynthesis. 2. Transient expression with AbPPAR produced PLA and abundant 2-O-malonylphenyllactate in N. benthamiana. 3. Combined with AbPMT2 and AbMPO2, it supported in planta formation of P1/P2 and several derivatives. 4. Substrate promiscuity may explain hydroxylated pyrrolidine variants.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream precursor-supply gene for heterologous production of PLA-derived alkaloids, but pathway engineering should consider its substrate promiscuity.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates putrescine to form N-methylputrescine, which is subsequently oxidized to N-methylaminobutanal and spontaneously cyclizes to N-methyl-Δ1-pyrrolinium.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supplies the N-methyl-Δ1-pyrrolinium precursor central to tropane and pyrrolidine scaffold formation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves in transient N. benthamiana assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Coexpression with AbMPO2 and aromatic precursor genes enables production of N-methyl-Δ1-pyrrolinium-dependent pyrrolidines P1 and P2 in N. benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression in N. benthamiana with metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Generation of reactive N-methyl-Δ1-pyrrolinium may cause unwanted byproducts during engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics (LC-MS, LC-MS/MS); data available on request
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously shown to support N-methyl-Δ1-pyrrolinium formation in N. benthamiana when coexpressed with AbMPO2.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AbPMT2 provides the methylated polyamine precursor for pyrrolinium formation. 2. In transient assays it contributed to in planta generation of P1/P2 when combined with AbMPO2, AbArAT4, and AbPPAR. 3. Its function supports both tropane alkaloid and novel pyrrolidine scaffold biosynthesis. 4. Engineering this step may increase reactive intermediate pools and off-target chemistry.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable for heterologous alkaloid scaffold production, but should be balanced to limit accumulation of reactive intermediates and byproducts.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: N-methylaminobutanal leading spontaneously to N-methyl-Δ1-pyrrolinium cation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes N-methylputrescine to N-methylaminobutanal, which spontaneously cyclizes to the reactive N-methyl-Δ1-pyrrolinium cation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Generates the electrophilic pyrrolinium precursor for both tropane and pyrrolidine alkaloid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves in transient N. benthamiana assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Coexpression with AbPMT2 and aromatic precursor genes enables formation of N-methyl-Δ1-pyrrolinium-dependent pyrrolidines in N. benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression in N. benthamiana with LC-MS/MS metabolite detection
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Formation of a highly electrophilic intermediate may increase off-target reactions and undesired alkaloid byproducts.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics (LC-MS, LC-MS/MS); data available on request
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The product N-methylaminobutanal spontaneously condenses intramolecularly to N-methyl-Δ1-pyrrolinium; the paper emphasizes downstream nonenzymatic reactivity of this cation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AbMPO2 generates the immediate precursor of N-methyl-Δ1-pyrrolinium. 2. In transient expression assays it was necessary, with AbPMT2, for pyrrolinium-dependent P1/P2 formation. 3. Its activity links polyamine metabolism to both tropane and newly uncovered pyrrolidine alkaloid networks. 4. The reactive cation generated downstream can drive nonenzymatic diversification.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important for synthetic biology of tropane/pyrrolidine alkaloids, but product reactivity necessitates careful control to minimize off-target metabolite formation.
